Hainan Meilan International Airport Company ( (HK:0357) ) has provided an update.

Hainan Meilan International Airport Company has elected Tian Qingquan as its employee director, effective 28 April 2026, following a democratic vote by staff representatives in line with its articles of association. Tian, aged 49, currently serves as general manager of the company’s planning and finance department and brings extensive financial and managerial experience from prior roles across HNA Group-related aviation, real estate and commercial entities.

His term will run until the end of the board’s ninth session, and his remuneration will be based solely on his existing executive position, with no additional director fees. The appointment, which does not require shareholder approval, enhances employee representation on the board while maintaining continuity in financial oversight, and leaves the board composed of three executive, three non-executive, four independent non-executive and one employee director.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Air China ( (HK:0753) ) has shared an announcement.

Air China has approved new financial services agreements with its 51%-owned subsidiary China National Aviation Finance (CNAF) and related parties to replace existing arrangements that expire at the end of 2026. Under the new deals, CNAF will continue to provide a range of financial services to Air China, its parent China National Aviation Holding Corporation (CNAHC) and Air China Cargo for three years from 1 January 2027 to 31 December 2029, with revised annual transaction caps and credit lines set by the board.

Because CNAF is a connected subsidiary, the transactions fall under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules as both discloseable and continuing connected transactions, triggering reporting, annual review, announcement and independent shareholder approval requirements. Air China plans to seek approval at its annual general meeting and will issue a circular detailing the arrangements and independent advice, underscoring governance procedures around intra-group financing and the airline’s reliance on centralized financial services within its corporate group.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0753) stock is a Sell with a HK$4.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Air China stock, see the HK:0753 Stock Forecast page.

More about Air China

Air China is a major Chinese airline operating as a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. Through its group entities, including Air China Cargo, it focuses on passenger and cargo air transport and related aviation services, and maintains integrated financial arrangements with its aviation-focused affiliates.

HG Semiconductor Limited ( (HK:6908) ) has shared an announcement.

HG Semiconductor Limited plans to amend its memorandum and articles of association to support a paperless regime, including the ability to hold hybrid and electronic general meetings and enable electronic voting by shareholders. The changes also aim to align the company with the latest Hong Kong listing rules on electronic instructions and payments, permit the holding and disposal of treasury shares, and prepare for the future uncertificated securities market regime.

The proposed amendments will be put to shareholders for approval by special resolution at the company’s annual general meeting scheduled for 27 May 2026. A circular detailing the revised governance documents and convening the meeting will be sent to shareholders, signaling an operational shift toward more digital, flexible capital management and enhanced compliance with market infrastructure developments.

Angang Steel Co ( (HK:0347) ) has shared an announcement.

Angang Steel reported unaudited first-quarter 2026 results showing operating income of RMB 22.02 billion, down 12.19% from a year earlier, as market pressures weighed on revenue. The company’s net loss attributable to shareholders widened sharply to RMB 1.46 billion, with basic and diluted earnings per share falling to a negative RMB 0.156 and weighted average return on net assets dropping to -3.38%, underscoring deteriorating profitability despite a 22.57% improvement in net cash flows from operating activities.

Management affirmed the truthfulness and completeness of the quarterly figures, noting that the report is unaudited but free of non-standard auditor opinions. The deepening losses and weaker margins highlight ongoing operational challenges for Angang Steel and may heighten concern among investors and other stakeholders about the company’s short-term financial performance and competitive position in a difficult steel market environment.

Renze Harvest International Limited has called its annual general meeting for 8 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will be asked to receive and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditors’ reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. Shanghai Electric Group Company Limited has formalized detailed rules of procedure for its Board Strategy Committee, defining its mandate to research and recommend the company’s long-term development strategy, plans, and major asset restructuring proposals requiring board approval. data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Voyah Automotive Technology Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:7489) ).

Voyah Automotive Technology Co., Ltd. has clarified that it is exempt from the usual Hong Kong listing requirement to issue a separate 2025 annual report to shareholders. The exemption applies because the company already included its full-year 2025 financial information and required corporate governance disclosures in its February 2026 Hong Kong listing document.

As a result, Voyah will not prepare or publish a standalone annual or summary financial report for the year ended December 31, 2025, relying instead on the information contained in the prospectus. The move streamlines compliance and reporting for its first post-listing period, while shareholders and potential investors are directed to access the 2025 financial results via the Hong Kong exchange website and the company’s own prospectus portal.

An announcement from China Hongqiao Group Ltd. ( (HK:1378) ) is now available.

Shandong Hongqiao New Material Co., Ltd., a key subsidiary of China Hongqiao Group Limited, has disclosed its unaudited consolidated balance sheet and income statement for the first quarter of 2026, prepared under PRC generally accepted accounting principles. The filing, translated from Chinese for investors, shows total assets of about RMB 224.4 billion and total shareholders’ equity of roughly RMB 131.9 billion, but the company emphasizes that these figures are based on internal management accounts and have not been reviewed or audited by external auditors.

The announcement highlights a reduction in total liabilities to around RMB 92.6 billion from RMB 101.4 billion at the beginning of the period, alongside an increase in equity attributable to the parent, suggesting some deleveraging and strengthened capital position at the subsidiary level. However, the board cautions investors not to place undue reliance on the first-quarter financial data and to exercise care when dealing in the company’s shares, underlining that the disclosure is voluntary and aimed at improving transparency for bond and equity market stakeholders.

data.title: South China Vocational Education Group Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Results and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

South China Vocational Education Group Co., Ltd. ( (HK:6913) ) just unveiled an update.

South China Vocational Education Group Company Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Guangzhou, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the accompanying board and auditor reports. The meeting will also consider re-electing one executive and two independent non-executive directors, reappointing AOGB CPA Limited as auditor, and granting the board a refreshed mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a move that preserves financing flexibility and could influence future capital-raising plans and governance continuity.

If approved, the director re-elections and auditor reappointment would maintain management and oversight stability at a time when regulatory compliance and educational quality are crucial for listed vocational providers in China. The proposed general mandate for new share issuance, excluding rights issues, employee schemes, and certain existing instruments, would give the board broad but time-limited authority to tap equity markets, potentially affecting shareholder dilution, capital structure, and the company’s capacity to fund expansion or strategic initiatives in the competitive vocational education sector.

More about South China Vocational Education Group Co., Ltd.

South China Vocational Education Group Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated education provider listed in Hong Kong, focusing on vocational education services in South China. The group operates through subsidiaries and is subject to the Listing Rules of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, reflecting its positioning within the regulated higher education and training sector.

data.title: Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable posts surging Q1 2026 profit and cash flow
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Co ( (HK:6869) ) just unveiled an update.

Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Limited Company reported a strong start to 2026, with first-quarter operating income rising 27.7% year on year to RMB 3.70 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders surging 226.4% to RMB 495.1 million. Excluding non-recurring items, net profit jumped more than ninefold, basic earnings per share tripled to RMB 0.60, and operating cash flow increased 41.9%, pointing to a substantial improvement in core profitability and cash generation despite largely flat total assets, which together suggest strengthened operational efficiency and an enhanced financial position for shareholders in the near term.

The unaudited figures, prepared under Chinese accounting standards and approved by the board, also show a 3.09% rise in total equity attributable to shareholders and an increase in weighted average return on equity to 3.53%, up 2.38 percentage points from a year earlier. This combination of higher margins, stronger cash inflows and improving returns indicates that the group is consolidating its competitive footing in the optical communications sector, potentially bolstering its capacity to invest in future growth and weather market volatility.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6869) stock is a Hold with a HK$255.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Co stock, see the HK:6869 Stock Forecast page.

More about Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Co

Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Limited Company is a PRC-incorporated manufacturer in the optical fibre and cable industry, supplying optical communication products to telecommunications and related infrastructure markets. Listed in Hong Kong, the group focuses on fibre and cable solutions that support network deployment and data transmission demands in China and internationally.
